[I-278](/wiki/Interstate_278_(New_York) "Interstate 278 (New York)") / [Manhattan Bridge](/wiki/Manhattan_Bridge "Manhattan Bridge") in [Downtown Brooklyn](/wiki/Downtown_Brooklyn "Downtown Brooklyn")

is a major avenue in the [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") [Borough](/wiki/Borough_(New_York_City) "Borough (New York City)") of [Brooklyn](/wiki/Brooklyn "Brooklyn"). It runs from the [Manhattan Bridge](/wiki/Manhattan_Bridge "Manhattan Bridge") south-southeastward to [Jamaica Bay](/wiki/Jamaica_Bay "Jamaica Bay"), where it joins the [Marine Parkway–Gil Hodges Memorial Bridge](/wiki/Marine_Parkway%E2%80%93Gil_Hodges_Memorial_Bridge "Marine Parkway–Gil Hodges Memorial Bridge"), which connects Brooklyn to the [Rockaway Peninsula](/wiki/Rockaway,_Queens "Rockaway, Queens") in [Queens](/wiki/Queens "Queens"). The north end was extended from [Fulton Street](/wiki/Fulton_Street_(Brooklyn) "Fulton Street (Brooklyn)") to the Manhattan Bridge as "Flatbush Avenue Extension".[[2]](#cite_note-2)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/81/Flatbush_%26_Beverly_2008_jeh.jpg/250px-Flatbush_%26_Beverly_2008_jeh.jpg)](/wiki/File:Flatbush_%26_Beverly_2008_jeh.jpg)

Beverly Road shopping area, looking north past [Kings Theatre](/wiki/Kings_Theatre_(Brooklyn,_New_York) "Kings Theatre (Brooklyn, New York)") towards Erasmus Hall

**Flatbush Avenue** is a major avenue in the [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") [Borough](/wiki/Borough_(New_York_City) "Borough (New York City)") of [Brooklyn](/wiki/Brooklyn "Brooklyn"). It runs from the [Manhattan Bridge](/wiki/Manhattan_Bridge "Manhattan Bridge") south-southeastward to [Jamaica Bay](/wiki/Jamaica_Bay "Jamaica Bay"), where it joins the [Marine Parkway–Gil Hodges Memorial Bridge](/wiki/Marine_Parkway%E2%80%93Gil_Hodges_Memorial_Bridge "Marine Parkway–Gil Hodges Memorial Bridge"), which connects Brooklyn to the [Rockaway Peninsula](/wiki/Rockaway,_Queens "Rockaway, Queens") in [Queens](/wiki/Queens "Queens"). The north end was extended from [Fulton Street](/wiki/Fulton_Street_(Brooklyn) "Fulton Street (Brooklyn)") to the Manhattan Bridge as "Flatbush Avenue Extension".[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Flatbush Avenue, including the extension, is 9.9 miles (15.9 km) long. The avenue is a four-lane street throughout the majority of its run. North of [Atlantic Avenue](/wiki/Atlantic_Avenue_(New_York_City) "Atlantic Avenue (New York City)") and south of [Utica Avenue](/wiki/Utica_Avenue "Utica Avenue"), it is a six-lane-wide median-divided street.

## Effect on street grid

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Flatbush_Avenue&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Effect on street grid")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This section **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Flatbush_Avenue "Special:EditPage/Flatbush Avenue") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners") in this section. Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *(January 2023)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

The diagonal path of Flatbush Avenue creates a unique street pattern in every neighborhood it touches. It is the central artery of the borough, carrying traffic to and from [Manhattan](/wiki/Manhattan "Manhattan") past landmarks such as [MetroTech Center](/wiki/MetroTech_Center "MetroTech Center"), [City Point](/wiki/City_Point_(Brooklyn) "City Point (Brooklyn)"), the [Fulton Mall](/wiki/Fulton_Mall "Fulton Mall"), [Junior's](/wiki/Junior%27s "Junior's"), [Long Island University](/wiki/Long_Island_University "Long Island University") [Brooklyn](/wiki/LIU_Brooklyn "LIU Brooklyn"), the [Brooklyn Academy of Music](/wiki/Brooklyn_Academy_of_Music "Brooklyn Academy of Music"), the [Long Island Rail Road](/wiki/Long_Island_Rail_Road "Long Island Rail Road")'s [Atlantic Terminal](/wiki/Atlantic_Terminal "Atlantic Terminal"), the [Barclays Center](/wiki/Barclays_Center "Barclays Center"), [Grand Army Plaza](/wiki/Grand_Army_Plaza "Grand Army Plaza"), the [Brooklyn Public Library](/wiki/Brooklyn_Public_Library "Brooklyn Public Library"), the [Brooklyn Botanic Garden](/wiki/Brooklyn_Botanic_Garden "Brooklyn Botanic Garden"), [Prospect Park](/wiki/Prospect_Park,_Brooklyn,_New_York "Prospect Park, Brooklyn, New York"), [Erasmus Hall High School](/wiki/Erasmus_Hall_High_School "Erasmus Hall High School"), [Kings Theatre](/wiki/Kings_Theatre_(Brooklyn) "Kings Theatre (Brooklyn)"), [Brooklyn College](/wiki/Brooklyn_College "Brooklyn College"), [Kings Plaza](/wiki/Kings_Plaza "Kings Plaza"), and [Floyd Bennett Field](/wiki/Floyd_Bennett_Field "Floyd Bennett Field").

Flatbush Avenue is the border of [Prospect Heights](/wiki/Prospect_Heights,_Brooklyn "Prospect Heights, Brooklyn")/[Park Slope](/wiki/Park_Slope,_Brooklyn "Park Slope, Brooklyn") and many other neighborhoods. Other main Brooklyn thoroughfares start at Flatbush Avenue, including [Ocean Avenue](/wiki/Ocean_Avenue_(Brooklyn) "Ocean Avenue (Brooklyn)") and Empire Boulevard (both at [Willink Plaza](/wiki/Willink_Plaza "Willink Plaza")), [Linden Boulevard](/wiki/Linden_Boulevard "Linden Boulevard"), [Eastern Parkway](/wiki/Eastern_Parkway "Eastern Parkway"), and Utica Avenue.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Flatbush_Avenue&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

Prior to European settlement, several [Native American trails](/wiki/Great_Trail "Great Trail") crossed Brooklyn.[[3]](#cite_note-Bolton_2018-3) These were later widened into "ferry roads" by 17th-century Dutch settlers, since they were used to provide transport to the waterfront. One was the Flatbush Road, running roughly north–south to the east of the path of present-day Flatbush Avenue.[[4]](#cite_note-Armbruster_1919_p.-4) The road ran roughly along what is now the eastern edge of Prospect Park and taking advantage of a low point in the [Heights of Guan](/wiki/Heights_of_Guan "Heights of Guan") that form the spine of [Long Island](/wiki/Long_Island "Long Island"). A monument beside the former Flatbush Road, now inside the park, commemorates an attempt to block the road at [Battle Pass](/wiki/Battle_Pass_(Brooklyn) "Battle Pass (Brooklyn)") during the [Battle of Long Island](/wiki/Battle_of_Long_Island "Battle of Long Island"). For much of the 19th century, it had a [plank road](/wiki/Plank_road "Plank road") run by a [turnpike](/wiki/Toll_road "Toll road") company. Historic homes line the neighborhoods around the avenue, which in the late 1920s was straightened to its current form. Streets such as Amersfort Place that are remnants of old parts of the avenue remain in the city grid as an echo of the past.

## Transportation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Flatbush_Avenue&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Transportation")]

Flatbush Avenue is served by the following [MTA Regional Bus Operations](/wiki/MTA_Regional_Bus_Operations "MTA Regional Bus Operations") routes:[[5]](#cite_note-5)

* The [B41](/wiki/B41_(New_York_City_bus) "B41 (New York City bus)") serves the majority of the avenue, between Livingston Street and either Avenue V, where [Kings Plaza](/wiki/Kings_Plaza "Kings Plaza") is located, or Avenue N, to serve [Bergen Beach](/wiki/Bergen_Beach "Bergen Beach").
* The [Q35](/wiki/Q35_(New_York_City_bus) "Q35 (New York City bus)") runs south of Avenue I ([Brooklyn College](/wiki/Brooklyn_College "Brooklyn College")), or Avenue H ([Rockaway Park, Queens](/wiki/Rockaway_Park,_Queens "Rockaway Park, Queens")).
* From Kings Plaza heading north, where all three terminate, the [B46](/wiki/B46_(New_York_City_bus) "B46 (New York City bus)") and [B46 SBS](/wiki/Select_Bus_Service#B46 "Select Bus Service") go to [Utica Avenue](/wiki/Utica_Avenue "Utica Avenue"), the [B2](/wiki/B2_(New_York_City_bus) "B2 (New York City bus)") goes to Avenue S, and [B9](/wiki/B9_(New_York_City_bus) "B9 (New York City bus)") buses that serve the full route go to Avenue L.
* The [B82](/wiki/B82_(New_York_City_bus) "B82 (New York City bus)") local runs on the avenue between Kings Highway and Flatlands Avenue. The [B82 SBS](/wiki/Select_Bus_Service#B82 "Select Bus Service") does not serve any portion.
* Northbound [B44 SBS](/wiki/Select_Bus_Service#B44 "Select Bus Service") buses run from [Nostrand Avenue](/wiki/Nostrand_Avenue "Nostrand Avenue") to Rogers Avenue. The [B44](/wiki/B44_(New_York_City_bus) "B44 (New York City bus)") local does not serve any portion.
* When serving the full route, the [B103](/wiki/B103_(New_York_City_bus) "B103 (New York City bus)") runs on two portions of Flatbush. One is between Avenue H and either terminates at Nostrand Avenue (Brooklyn College), or continues to Cortelyou Road ([Downtown Brooklyn](/wiki/Downtown_Brooklyn "Downtown Brooklyn")), where it's accompanied by the [BM2](/wiki/BM2_(New_York_City_bus) "BM2 (New York City bus)") express. The other portion is between Livingston Street and either Lafayette Avenue (Downtown Brooklyn), or 4th Avenue ([Canarsie](/wiki/Canarsie,_Brooklyn "Canarsie, Brooklyn")).
* The [B11](/wiki/B11_(New_York_City_bus) "B11 (New York City bus)") runs between Glenwood Road and Brooklyn College, where all buses terminate. The [B6](/wiki/B6_(New_York_City_bus) "B6 (New York City bus)") uses the avenue to continue along Glenwood, past East 29th Street.
* Prospect Park-bound [B16](/wiki/B16_(New_York_City_bus) "B16 (New York City bus)") buses run from Ocean Avenue to Lincoln Road.
* From 7th Avenue, the [B67](/wiki/B67_(New_York_City_bus) "B67 (New York City bus)") heads north to Livingston Street, while the [B69](/wiki/B69_(New_York_City_bus) "B69 (New York City bus)") heads south to Plaza Street East.
* The [B63](/wiki/B63_(New_York_City_bus) "B63 (New York City bus)") runs between 5th and [Atlantic Avenues](/wiki/Atlantic_Avenue_(New_York_City) "Atlantic Avenue (New York City)"), west from Flatbush. The [B45](/wiki/B45_(New_York_City_bus) "B45 (New York City bus)") runs between Livingston Street and Atlantic, east from Flatbush.
* [Ridgewood](/wiki/Ridgewood,_Queens "Ridgewood, Queens")-bound [B54](/wiki/B54_(New_York_City_bus) "B54 (New York City bus)") buses run on the Extension part of the avenue from Tillary Street to [Myrtle Avenue](/wiki/Myrtle_Avenue "Myrtle Avenue").

A dedicated [bus lane corridor](/wiki/Bus_lanes_in_New_York_City "Bus lanes in New York City") on Flatbush Avenue was proposed in 2022;[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7) at the time, the B41 route traveled at an average speed of 6.5 miles per hour (10.5 km/h).[[8]](#cite_note-8) Designs for the bus lanes were revealed in 2024,[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10) and the [New York City Department of Transportation](/wiki/New_York_City_Department_of_Transportation "New York City Department of Transportation") announced the next year that center-running bus lanes, with pedestrian islands, would be built on the northern part of the avenue from Livingston Street to Grand Army Plaza.[[11]](#cite_note-11) The lanes are to be installed starting in late 2025.[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13)

Flatbush Avenue is served by the following [New York City Subway](/wiki/New_York_City_Subway "New York City Subway") stations:

* The [IRT Nostrand Avenue Line](/wiki/IRT_Nostrand_Avenue_Line "IRT Nostrand Avenue Line") ([2](/wiki/2_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "2 (New York City Subway service)") and ​[5](/wiki/5_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "5 (New York City Subway service)") trains) has a southern terminus at the [Flatbush Avenue–Brooklyn College](/wiki/Flatbush_Avenue%E2%80%93Brooklyn_College_(IRT_Nostrand_Avenue_Line) "Flatbush Avenue–Brooklyn College (IRT Nostrand Avenue Line)") station.
* The [BMT Brighton Line](/wiki/BMT_Brighton_Line "BMT Brighton Line") ([B](/wiki/B_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "B (New York City Subway service)") and ​[Q](/wiki/Q_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "Q (New York City Subway service)") trains) and [IRT Eastern Parkway Line](/wiki/IRT_Eastern_Parkway_Line "IRT Eastern Parkway Line") ([2](/wiki/2_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "2 (New York City Subway service)"), ​[3](/wiki/3_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "3 (New York City Subway service)"), ​[4](/wiki/4_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "4 (New York City Subway service)"), and ​[5](/wiki/5_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "5 (New York City Subway service)") trains) run under the avenue between [Prospect Park](/wiki/Prospect_Park_(BMT_Brighton_Line) "Prospect Park (BMT Brighton Line)") and [DeKalb Avenue](/wiki/DeKalb_Avenue_(BMT_Brighton_Line) "DeKalb Avenue (BMT Brighton Line)"), and [Grand Army Plaza](/wiki/Grand_Army_Plaza_(IRT_Eastern_Parkway_Line) "Grand Army Plaza (IRT Eastern Parkway Line)") and [Nevins Street](/wiki/Nevins_Street_(IRT_Eastern_Parkway_Line) "Nevins Street (IRT Eastern Parkway Line)"), respectively.[[14]](#cite_note-14)
